channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Christopher Nolan praises Samantha Morton’s role in 'The Odyssey' as he recalls Heath Ledger’s legacy
item.description: Christopher Nolan has lauded Samantha Morton's performance in his new film adaptation of Homer's 'The Odyssey', drawing a parallel to the late Heath Ledger's iconic portrayal in 'The Dark Knight'. The...

item.title: BC Parks Launches Fundraising Campaign to Acquire Private Parcel for Myra Bellevue Park Conservation
item.description: The BC Parks Foundation has initiated a fundraising campaign to acquire a 32-hectare privately-owned parcel within Myra Bellevue Provincial Park, aiming to protect a critical biodiversity hotspot and ...

item.title: Kenyan Woman Graduates as US Air Force Medical Technician After Joining Military
item.description: Shiko Hope Wa Njeri, a Kenyan woman working in the US Air Force, has graduated from the Basic Medical Technician Corpsman Program at Fort Sam Houston, Texas. She moved to the US via the Diversity Visa...

item.title: Tragic head-on collision on N2 claims five lives near Heidelberg
item.description: A devastating head-on collision on the N2 near Heidelberg on Tuesday morning resulted in the deaths of five individuals, including a 13-year-old boy, his 20-year-old brother, their parents, and Michel...

item.title: Israeli strike kills Gaza aid worker during World Cup screening event
item.description: An Israeli strike in Gaza killed Mohamed al-Wahidi, a top Palestinian aid official working with Egypt's relief arm, during a FIFA World Cup screening event organized for the Egypt-Argentina match. The...

item.title: Nigel Farage Resigns as MP for Clacton, Plans to Run Again
item.description: Nigel Farage, the UK Independence Party leader, has announced his resignation as MP for Clacton, triggering a by-election. Despite claims of seeking to challenge 'the establishment' and clear his name...

item.title: FIFA Investigates Racist Abuse Against US Streamer IShowSpeed
item.description: The International Football Federation (FIFA) has launched an investigation into reports of racist abuse directed at US content creator and streamer IShowSpeed. The probe follows allegations that the s...

item.title: Controversial Lawyer Brian Mwenda Purchases High-Value SUV
item.description: Brian Mwenda, the controversial lawyer at the center of a fake credentials scandal, has sparked online reactions after showcasing a brand new Land Rover Defender worth over KSh17 million. The video re...

item.title: US-Iran Escalation: Strikes and Retaliation Over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The article details the escalating conflict between the United States and Iran, triggered by US military strikes on Iran to secure the Strait of Hormuz. These strikes, aimed at preventing Iranian atta...

item.title: Senate Commences Hearings on School Safety Act Addressing Violence and Bullying
item.description: The Senate of the Philippines began hearings on the proposed School Safety Act on July 9, 2026, focusing on addressing violence, bullying, and other threats in and around schools. The hearings aim to ...

item.title: Kaduna Businessman Files Lawsuit Against Sheikh Gumi and Others Over Alleged Detention and Theft
item.description: A Kaduna businessman, Nasir Musa Idris, has filed multiple criminal complaints against Islamic scholar Dr. Ahmad Abubakar Gumi and six others, alleging wrongful detention of his Moroccan wife, theft o...

item.title: Junior Andre shares emotional struggle over childhood neglect by Katie Price
item.description: Junior Andre, the son of reality TV star Katie Price, has opened up about his emotional turmoil stemming from feeling neglected during his childhood. In a candid interview, the 21-year-old revealed th...

item.title: UN Secretary-General Commends Pakistan's Peace Efforts During Summit Meeting
item.description: United Nations Secretary General Antonio Guterres praised Pakistan's peace initiatives during a meeting with Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi at the fifth United Nations Chiefs of Police Summit (UNCOPS ...

item.title: Winnipeg Blue Bombers QB Zach Collaros Likely Out for Game Against Argonauts
item.description: The Winnipeg Blue Bombers are facing uncertainty at quarterback as Zach Collaros remains questionable for Friday’s matchup against the Toronto Argonauts. Collaros exited Sunday’s game after sustaining...

item.title: DC Fountain Turns Brown After Renovation Work Sparks Controversy
item.description: The iconic cascading fountain in Meridian Hill Park, also known as Malcolm X Park, turned a thick, opaque brown just months after a $16 million renovation led by President Donald Trump. The discolorat...

item.title: Jim Iyke Criticizes Nollywood for Promoting Satanic Values Over Religious Ones
item.description: Veteran actor Jim Iyke made bold claims about the Nigerian entertainment industry during an interview, stating it's designed to glorify Satan rather than God. He argued that fame and self-glorificatio...

item.title: Maine Senate candidate exits race amid sexual assault allegations and political fallout
item.description: Graham Platner, a Maine oysterman and former military veteran, has withdrawn from the Senate race against Republican Senator Susan Collins following allegations of sexual assault. The decision comes a...

item.title: Set-Jetting Travel Trend: Planning a Trip to Northern Ireland
item.description: The article explores the growing phenomenon of 'set-jetting,' where travelers visit locations featured in popular media, such as the fictional Winterfell from *Game of Thrones*. It highlights Northern...

item.title: Sarah Storey announces retirement at 48: Reflecting on a legendary Paralympic career
item.description: Sarah Storey, Britain's most decorated Paralympian with 74 medals across swimming and cycling, has announced her retirement from elite competition at the age of 48. The article details her remarkable ...

item.title: Alexander Zverev advances to Wimbledon semi-finals after defeating Taylor Fritz
item.description: German tennis star Alexander Zverev secured his first Wimbledon semi-final berth by defeating American Taylor Fritz in a three-set match on Wednesday. The second-seeded player, who had previously stru...
